title: lavandula vera var. pyrenaica
intro: Lavandula vera var. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean lavender, is a subspecies of lavender native to the Pyrenees mountain range between France and Spain. This plant is valued for its calming properties and is used in herbal medicine to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The medicinal properties of Lavandula vera var. pyrenaica include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and muscle spasms. Herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and topical creams can be made from the plant, and are often used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce stress levels.
